contract_meta.rationale: Phase 4 split into two contracts with smaller wins and validation checkpoints. 4A builds task decomposition foundation: planning contracts, decomposer, validator, progress tracker. Includes pre/post validation to prevent building wrong solutions.
contract_meta.architectural_note: Phase 4 introduces system making architectural decisions (decomposition strategy, subtask ordering). Validation checkpoints critical to ensure correctness.

project.name: executor-mvp
project.goal: Build foundation for task planning: decompose complex requests into subtasks, validate decompositions, track execution progress. System can break 'todo app with auth' into 5 subtasks and show progress.
project.scope: Foundation wins only - contracts, decomposer, validator, progress tracker
project.estimated_time: 5-6 wins over 2-3 weeks at sustainable pace
project.work_profile: Backend planning logic, validation, data structures - no UI yet, no full integration yet

execution_model.description: Agent verifies Phase 3 completion, builds planning foundation with mandatory validation after each win. Pre-validation ensures we're building the right thing. Post-validation ensures it works correctly.

validation_protocol.pre_validation: Before implementing win, validate approach against real use cases
validation_protocol.post_validation: After implementing win, validate output quality against examples

observability.description: Enhanced observability for planning decisions and decomposition quality

validation.pre_implementation: Validate approach with 3-5 real examples before coding
validation.post_implementation: Validate output quality with 5-10 test cases
validation.checkpoint_approval: Developer must approve validation results before proceeding

tasks.title: Verify Phase 3 completion
tasks.description: Confirm Phase 3 (Multi-turn repair) is complete before starting Phase 4A planning foundation

evaluation.intent: Verify Phase 3 multi-turn repair complete before planning foundation
evaluation.success_criteria: All Phase 3 artifacts exist and tests pass
evaluation.on_fail: HALT - Phase 3 must be complete before Phase 4A

tasks.title: Pre-Validation: Task Plan Contract Design
tasks.description: Before implementing, validate task plan structure against real use cases

validation_questions: What does a task plan look like for 'build todo app with auth'?
What fields are needed: subtask name, description, dependencies, estimated complexity?
How do we represent dependencies: array of subtask IDs? Ordered list?
What validation rules: minimum 2 subtasks, maximum 10, unique IDs?
Should we include success criteria per subtask?
example_use_cases: Medium: 'build todo app with auth' -> 5-7 subtasks
validation_criteria: Validation rules prevent degenerate cases (1 subtask, circular deps)

approval_required.developer_must_review: Example task plans for all use cases
approval_required.approval_criteria: Structure makes sense, validation rules adequate

tasks.title: Win #28: Define Task Plan Contracts
tasks.description: Create task-plan.schema.json and subtask.schema.json for decomposed task representation
tasks.work_profile: Schema definition based on validated structure

ensure: Add custom validation: no circular dependencies, dependency IDs exist in subtasks array

test_cases: valid medium plan (5-7 subtasks) with dependencies
invalid: dependency references non-existent subtask ID

success_criteria: Validator enforces schema and custom rules

tasks.title: Post-Validation: Task Plan Contract Quality
tasks.description: After implementing, validate schemas work correctly with real examples

test_examples: Create task plan JSON for 'build Flask hello world'
Create task plan JSON for 'build todo app with auth'
Create task plan JSON for 'build e-commerce site'
Validate all examples pass schema validation
Validate circular dependency detection catches invalid cases
Validate dependency ID validation catches invalid cases
validation_criteria: Error messages are helpful and specific

approval_required.developer_must_review: Validation results for all test examples
approval_required.approval_criteria: Schemas work correctly, validation catches all edge cases
approval_required.on_rejection: Fix schema/validator issues before proceeding to next win

tasks.title: Pre-Validation: Decomposition Strategy
tasks.description: Before implementing decomposer, validate decomposition approach

validation_questions: What decomposition strategies exist: by-feature, by-layer, by-dependency?
How do we prompt LLM to decompose consistently?
What constraints: 2-10 subtasks, dependencies make sense, no circular deps?
How do we handle ambiguous prompts: 'build an app' vs 'build todo app with auth'?
Should we use clarification (Phase 2) before decomposition?
example_prompts: 'build todo app with auth' -> what subtasks?
'build REST API for blog' -> what subtasks?
validation_criteria: Number of subtasks appropriate to complexity

approval_required.approval_criteria: Strategy produces good decompositions, handles edge cases
approval_required.on_rejection: Revise strategy, add clarification step if needed

tasks.title: Win #29: Task Decomposer
tasks.description: Create decomposeTask.ts that breaks complex prompts into subtask plans
tasks.work_profile: LLM prompt engineering and task decomposition logic

content_spec.function_signature: export async function decomposeTask(prompt: string, clarifications?: ClarificationResponse): Promise<TaskPlan>
decomposition_logic: If ambiguous and no clarifications: suggest returning error with clarification recommendation
Build LLM prompt for decomposition using approved strategy
Include constraints: 2-10 subtasks, logical dependencies, actionable descriptions
error_handling: LLM returns invalid JSON: retry once
Validation fails: return error with details

test_cases: simple prompt: 'build Flask hello world' -> 2-3 subtasks
medium prompt: 'build todo app with auth' -> 5-7 subtasks
with clarifications: uses clarification data in decomposition

success_criteria: Decomposes prompts into valid task plans

tasks.title: Post-Validation: Decomposition Quality
tasks.description: After implementing, validate decomposition quality with real LLM

live_llm_tests: Review all decompositions for quality, dependency logic, subtask clarity
validation_criteria: Number of subtasks appropriate to complexity

approval_required.approval_criteria: Decomposition quality acceptable, no major issues
approval_required.on_rejection: Tune LLM prompt, adjust constraints, re-test

tasks.title: Pre-Validation: Decomposition Validation Approach
tasks.description: Before implementing validator, determine what makes a good decomposition

validation_questions: What quality metrics: subtask clarity, dependency logic, coverage completeness?
How do we detect bad decompositions: too vague, too granular, missing critical steps?
Should we validate against original prompt: does decomposition cover all requirements?
What automated checks: subtask count, dependency acyclic check, description length?
What human-review triggers: flag decompositions with potential issues?
example_bad_decompositions: Too vague: 'Step 1: Set up project, Step 2: Build features'
Too granular: 'Step 1: Create directory, Step 2: Install package, Step 3: Import library...'
Missing critical: Todo app without database step
Circular: Task A depends on B, B depends on A
validation_criteria: Validator catches all bad decomposition patterns

approval_required.on_rejection: Add more quality metrics, refine bad pattern detection

tasks.title: Win #30: Decomposition Validator
tasks.description: Create validateDecomposition.ts that scores decomposition quality

content_spec.function_signature: export function validateDecomposition(plan: TaskPlan, originalPrompt: string): DecompositionQuality
quality_checks: Coverage: does decomposition address original prompt
Granularity: not too vague, not too detailed
Critical steps present (database if data needed, auth if mentioned, tests always)

test_cases: good decomposition: high score, no issues
adequate but not perfect: warnings, medium score

success_criteria: Issues and warnings helpful and specific

tasks.title: Post-Validation: Validator Accuracy
tasks.description: After implementing, validate validator catches bad decompositions

test_decompositions: Create intentionally bad decompositions (too vague, missing steps, circular)
Verify validator catches all bad patterns
validation_criteria: Validator catches all intentional bad patterns

approval_required.approval_criteria: Validator accurate, catches issues, scoring reasonable
approval_required.on_rejection: Tune scoring weights, add more quality checks

tasks.title: Win #31: Progress Tracker
tasks.description: Create progress tracker that monitors subtask execution status
success_criteria: Respects dependencies when getting next subtask

demo_requirement.manual_test: Create task plan, simulate subtask execution, verify progress tracking

tasks.title: Win #32: Dependency Analyzer
tasks.description: Create dependency analyzer that validates and visualizes subtask dependencies

return_type.cycles: array of subtask ID arrays forming cycles
return_type.parallelizable: array of subtask sets that can run in parallel
return_type.criticalPath: array of subtask IDs on longest dependency chain

analysis_logic: Build dependency graph from task plan
Compute topological sort for execution order

demo_requirement.manual_test: Analyze W29 decompositions, verify dependency analysis correct

tasks.title: Phase 4A validation gate
tasks.description: Final validation that Phase 4A planning foundation is complete before moving to Phase 4B (execution)

actions.cmd: test -f src/planning/decomposeTask.ts && test -f src/planning/validateDecomposition.ts && test -f src/planning/progressTracker.ts && test -f src/planning/analyzeDependencies.ts

test_scenarios: Verify dependency analysis correct for all

evaluation.intent: Confirm Phase 4A foundation is production-ready before Phase 4B execution
evaluation.success_criteria: All tests pass, all validation checkpoints approved, decomposition quality good, dependency analysis accurate
evaluation.on_pass: Phase 4A COMPLETE - Ready for Phase 4B (sequential execution and UI)
evaluation.on_fail: HALT - Fix issues before proceeding to Phase 4B

win_isolation.description: Each win must be complete, tested, and validated before proceeding
win_isolation.implementation: Pre-validation before implementation, post-validation after implementation, developer approval at checkpoints

P4A-GATE.on_fail: HALT execution. Do not proceed to Phase 4B.
P4A-GATE.report: Generate diagnostic showing which validations failed.

fallback_policies.phase3_verification_fails: HALT - Phase 3 must be complete before Phase 4A.
fallback_policies.validation_checkpoint_fails: Stop at that checkpoint, address issues, re-validate before continuing.
fallback_policies.test_fails: Fix implementation, do not skip tests.
fallback_policies.coverage_drops: Add tests to maintain >80% coverage before proceeding.
fallback_policies.decomposition_quality_poor: Tune LLM prompts, adjust constraints, re-test.

release_actions.cmd: git commit -m "feat(phase4a): task planning foundation - decomposition, validation, progress tracking, dependency analysis"

ai_executor_guidelines.observability_first: Trace all decisions. Log reasoning for each win and validation checkpoint.
ai_executor_guidelines.validation_mandatory: Never skip pre/post validation checkpoints. Wait for developer approval.
ai_executor_guidelines.win_isolation: Complete and validate each win fully before proceeding to next.
ai_executor_guidelines.test_driven: Write comprehensive tests for all planning logic.
ai_executor_guidelines.fail_fast: If any test or validation fails, halt and fix before proceeding.
ai_executor_guidelines.gate_enforcement: P4A-GATE must pass before declaring phase complete.
ai_executor_guidelines.quality_focus: Decomposition quality is critical - poor decompositions make execution fail.

governance_note.for_human: Phase 4A builds planning foundation with mandatory validation checkpoints. Each win has pre-validation (validate approach) and post-validation (validate quality). Developer must approve validation results before proceeding. This ensures we build the right thing correctly. Estimated 2-3 weeks at sustainable pace. After P4A-GATE, assess with wife whether to proceed to Phase 4B or take break.
